(Latvian pronunciation: [[ɑːgɑːtɛ t͡saʊnɛ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Latvian "Help:IPA/Latvian") [*ah-GAH-teh tsown-eh*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key"); born 7 August 2004 in [Valmiera](/wiki/Valmiera "Valmiera"), [Latvia](/wiki/Latvia "Latvia")) is a Latvian [track and field](/wiki/Track_and_field "Track and field") athlete. A multiple national champion, she is also the Latvian national record holder over 3000 metres.[[3]](#cite_note-3) She was named the Rising Star of the Year[[4]](#cite_note-4) and the female U20 Track and Field Athlete of the Year in 2022.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Latvian middle - and long-distance runner (born 2004)

Agate Caune

**Agate Caune** (Latvian pronunciation: [[ɑːgɑːtɛ t͡saʊnɛ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Latvian "Help:IPA/Latvian") [*ah-GAH-teh tsown-eh*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key"); born 7 August 2004 in [Valmiera](/wiki/Valmiera "Valmiera"), [Latvia](/wiki/Latvia "Latvia")) is a Latvian [track and field](/wiki/Track_and_field "Track and field") athlete. A multiple national champion, she is also the Latvian national record holder over 3000 metres.[[3]](#cite_note-3) She was named the Rising Star of the Year[[4]](#cite_note-4) and the female U20 Track and Field Athlete of the Year in 2022.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Agate_Caune&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Agate_Caune&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early career")]

Caune started out in 2019 as the Latvian U16 champion in the 1500 metres and 3000 metres events. Gradually, she became the 2020 Latvian U18 champion in the indoor and outdoor 3000 metres. During the following 2021 Latvian U18 Championships, she won gold in the 800 metres and 1500 metres. Additionally, she gained her first national title in the 5000 metres the same year.

### 2021

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Agate_Caune&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2021")]

From [Valmiera](/wiki/Valmiera "Valmiera"), Caune won the silver medal in the [5000m](/wiki/5000m "5000m") at the [2021 European Athletics U20 Championships](/wiki/2021_European_Athletics_U20_Championships "2021 European Athletics U20 Championships") in [Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"), whilst just 16 years-old. She then went to the [2021 World Athletics U20 Championships](/wiki/2021_World_Athletics_U20_Championships "2021 World Athletics U20 Championships") in [Nairobi](/wiki/Nairobi "Nairobi") and finished fifth in the 3000m.

### 2022

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Agate_Caune&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2022")]

She was the national champion in the 5000 metres for the second time, as well as the Latvian 10,000 metres champion in 2022. Throughout the year, she was declared the Latvian U20 champion for distances such as the: indoor 800 metres, indoor and outdoor 1500 metres, and outdoor 3000 metres.

Competing at the [2022 World U20 Championships](/wiki/2022_World_Athletics_U20_Championships "2022 World Athletics U20 Championships"), held in [Cali](/wiki/Cali "Cali"), Colombia, she finished sixth in the 3000m final and ran 15:43.56 to win a silver medal in the 5000m (originally finished fourth behind [Medina Eisa](/wiki/Medina_Eisa "Medina Eisa") and [Melknat Wudu](/wiki/Melknat_Wudu "Melknat Wudu"), but Eisa and Wudu were later given a two-year ban for age falsification violations and stripped of their gold and silver medals).[[7]](#cite_note-7). Caune ran 15:12.94 for the 5000m in [Karlsruhe](/wiki/Karlsruhe "Karlsruhe"), Germany in May 2023.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

### 2023

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Agate_Caune&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2023")]

She remains the champion of the 2023 Latvian 10,000 metres. Currently, she has national titles in the outdoor 1500 metres and indoor 3000 metres. She received further awards at the 2023 Latvian U20 Championships when she won in categories like the indoor 800 metres and the indoor and outdoor 1500 metres.

At the [2023 European Indoor Championships](/wiki/2023_European_Athletics_Indoor_Championships "2023 European Athletics Indoor Championships") held in [Istanbul](/wiki/Istanbul "Istanbul") in March 2023, Caune qualified for the final, where she set a new Latvian U20 record for the 3000m, clocking 8:56.88 to finish in eleventh place.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

In May 2023, Caune won a 5k event at the [Riga Marathon](/wiki/Riga_Marathon "Riga Marathon"), running it in 15:23 seconds, beating the [Jeļena Prokopčuka](/wiki/Je%C4%BCena_Prokop%C4%8Duka "Jeļena Prokopčuka") best previous time from 2009, by seven seconds.[[12]](#cite_note-12) Caune then won a gold medal in the 5000 meters at the [2023 European Team Games](/wiki/2023_European_Athletics_Team_Championships "2023 European Athletics Team Championships") running 15:15.21 in the Second Division race.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

On July 16, 2023, in [Silesia](/wiki/Silesia "Silesia"), Caune made her [Diamond League](/wiki/2023_Diamond_League "2023 Diamond League") debut and finished in seventh place in the 3000m distance, running 8:39.78 to break the Latvian national record.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

In August 2023, Caune won gold medals in the 3000 metres and 5000 metres at the [2023 European Athletics U20 Championships](/wiki/2023_European_Athletics_U20_Championships "2023 European Athletics U20 Championships") in [Jerusalem](/wiki/Jerusalem "Jerusalem"). She set a new championship record of 15:03.38 in the 5000 metres.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16) Caune subsequently went on to the [2023 World Championships](/wiki/2023_World_Athletics_Championships "2023 World Athletics Championships") in [Budapest](/wiki/Budapest "Budapest"). She used a bold strategy—heading straight to the front of the pack and not slowing down as the distance between her and her competitors increased—that helped her advance to the next round of the 5000 metres. However, prior to the final event, Caune withdrew due to a pelvic injury that had been present from the past few days. Upon the recommendation of her doctors, she elected to miss the race and not risk worsening her condition if she were to run.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2a/Nadia_Battocletti_and_Ludovica_Cavalli.jpg/250px-Nadia_Battocletti_and_Ludovica_Cavalli.jpg)](/wiki/File:Nadia_Battocletti_and_Ludovica_Cavalli.jpg)

Caune (centre) at the [2023 European Indoor Championships](/wiki/2023_European_Athletics_Indoor_Championships "2023 European Athletics Indoor Championships")

### 2024

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Agate_Caune&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2024")]

She was selected to compete for Latvia at the [2024 Olympic](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2024_Summer_Olympics "Athletics at the 2024 Summer Olympics") [5000 meters](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2024_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_5000_metres "Athletics at the 2024 Summer Olympics – Women's 5000 metres") in [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"). She ran in the first heat Aug. 2. The first eight finishers qualified for the second round. Caune finished 16th in her heat (and 33rd overall) in a time of 15:38.19.[[18]](#cite_note-18)
